metadata.description: Complete control taxonomy for AI SAFE2 v3.0. Powers the AI SAFE2 MCP Server tools.

pillar_controls.name: Input Validation & Schema Enforcement
pillar_controls.description: Validate inputs against schemas; reject malformed formats; enforce type checking and content-length restrictions.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent accepts anything and crashes on edge cases.

pillar_controls.name: Malicious Prompt Filtering
pillar_controls.description: Detect and block prompt injection (OWASP LLM01); implement adversarial detection; monitor for jailbreaks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent follows instructions embedded in documents it retrieves.

pillar_controls.name: Data Quality Checks
pillar_controls.description: Validate data integrity; detect statistical anomalies and outliers; flag corrupted data.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent produces garbage outputs when input data is noisy.

pillar_controls.name: Toxic Content Detection
pillar_controls.description: Screen for harmful content; implement toxicity scoring; filter based on policy.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent outputs content that violates our policy.

pillar_controls.name: Sensitive Data Masking (PII/PHI)
pillar_controls.description: Auto-detect and mask PII; redact PHI (HIPAA); implement DLP controls; tokenize sensitive fields.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent leaks user PII in its outputs or logs.

pillar_controls.name: Format Normalization
pillar_controls.description: Standardize input formats; validate character encodings (UTF-8); prevent encoding attacks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent behaves differently with unicode vs ASCII input.

pillar_controls.name: Dependency Verification
pillar_controls.description: Validate software dependencies; maintain SBOMs; cross-reference CVE databases.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I do not know which vulnerable libraries my AI stack uses.

pillar_controls.name: Encoding Validation
pillar_controls.description: Enforce normalization forms (NFKC); prevent homoglyph and encoding-based attacks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Attackers bypass my filters using look-alike characters.

pillar_controls.name: Supply Chain Artifact Validation
pillar_controls.description: Cryptographically verify model and dataset authenticity; validate checksums (SHA-256); enforce signature verification.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I cannot prove the model I am running is the one I downloaded.

pillar_controls.name: Agent Sandboxing
pillar_controls.description: Deploy agents in isolated environments; implement resource limits (CPU/RAM); use containerization.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent can access resources beyond what its task requires.

pillar_controls.name: Network Segmentation
pillar_controls.description: Isolate AI workloads in dedicated VLANs; use firewalls and ACLs to restrict traffic.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My AI agents share a network with sensitive internal systems.

pillar_controls.name: API Gateway Restrictions
pillar_controls.description: Deploy gateways with auth; implement rate limiting; restrict access by IP/Role.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Any code that can reach my API can call my AI agents.

pillar_controls.name: Model Versioning & Isolation
pillar_controls.description: Maintain separate environments for model versions; isolate production from development; prevent unintended rollbacks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Development model changes are accidentally affecting production.

pillar_controls.name: Function Access Control
pillar_controls.description: Restrict agent access to tools; implement whitelisting; use least privilege.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent can call any tool it wants, far beyond what it needs for its task.

pillar_controls.name: Data Isolation
pillar_controls.description: Separate sensitive data; implement access controls; encrypt at rest and in transit.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Different tenants' data is mixed in our shared AI context.

pillar_controls.name: Container Security
pillar_controls.description: Harden container images (minimal base); scan for vulnerabilities; implement runtime monitoring.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our AI containers run as root with no vulnerability scanning.

pillar_controls.name: Firewall Controls
pillar_controls.description: Deploy NGFWs; implement IDS/IPS; enforce egress filtering.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My AI agent can exfiltrate data to any external endpoint.

pillar_controls.name: Credential Compartmentalization
pillar_controls.description: Store API keys in secure vaults (HashiCorp/AWS); rotate regularly; never in code or prompts.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: API keys for AI services are in environment variables or hardcoded in prompts.

pillar_controls.name: Supply Chain Artifact Validation (OpenSSF OMS)
pillar_controls.description: Cryptographic model verification using Sigstore/Cosign; SBOM validation; provenance chain; SHA-256 model fingerprinting.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My model could have been tampered with between training and deployment.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Secret Validation
pillar_controls.description: Detect embedded credentials/tokens in AI outputs; GitGuardian integration for real-time secret detection.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent is leaking API keys in its responses.

pillar_controls.name: Memory-Specific Attack Mitigation
pillar_controls.description: Cryptographic memory fingerprinting; thread injection prevention; semantic similarity analysis for gradual RAG poison.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent's behavior changed slowly over weeks and I cannot explain why.

pillar_controls.name: Multi-Agent Boundary Enforcement
pillar_controls.description: Isolate Agent-to-Agent communications; auto-quarantine anomalous agents; P2P trust scoring.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: One agent is influencing other agents in ways I did not design.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Access Control
pillar_controls.description: Catalog all service accounts and agents; assign minimum permissions to NHI entities; automated decommissioning.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agents have more permissions than they need.

pillar_controls.name: Indirect Injection Surface Coverage
pillar_controls.description: Enumerate every non-prompt input channel the agent processes and apply the same sanitization rigor as direct user input.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent follows instructions embedded in documents, emails, and tool outputs — not just user chat.

pillar_controls.name: Semantic Isolation Boundary Enforcement
pillar_controls.description: Architecturally separate trusted system instruction context from untrusted content processing at the inference layer.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent goes off-script mid-task because retrieved content is contaminating its instructions.

pillar_controls.name: Adversarial Input Fuzzing Pipeline
pillar_controls.description: Integrate automated adversarial testing into CI/CD — systematic attack probe battery before every model update and deployment.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent works in testing but breaks in production on inputs I did not think to test.

pillar_controls.name: Memory Governance Boundary Controls
pillar_controls.description: Every write to persistent agent memory requires authorization, sanitization with input-equivalent rigor, and an append-only audit log entry.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent remembers the wrong things and I cannot explain why its behavior changed after weeks of operation.

pillar_controls.name: Cognitive Injection Sanitization
pillar_controls.description: Semantic intent analysis layer that detects multi-turn behavioral conditioning, role confusion, and few-shot pattern implanting beyond pattern-matching filters.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My prompt injection filters pass obvious attacks but miss subtle multi-session manipulation.

pillar_controls.name: No-Code / Low-Code Platform Security
pillar_controls.description: First dedicated governance standard for no-code AI workflow platforms: sandbox isolation, credential scoping, template supply chain, CVE patching as P1.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I built AI workflows in n8n or Zapier and CVE-2026-25049 is being actively exploited.

pillar_controls.name: Real-Time Activity Logging
pillar_controls.description: Log all activities with timestamps; capture prompts and API calls; ensure tamper-proof storage.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I cannot tell what my agent did during the time it produced wrong outputs.

pillar_controls.name: Model Performance Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Monitor accuracy and precision; detect data and concept drift.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent's quality degraded silently after a corpus update.

pillar_controls.name: Behavior Verification
pillar_controls.description: Establish baselines; detect deviations and anomalies; flag unusual patterns.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent does something unexpected and I have no baseline to compare against.

pillar_controls.name: Explainability Tracking
pillar_controls.description: Log decisions (SHAP/LIME); track reasoning paths; enable post-hoc analysis.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My stakeholders ask why the agent made a decision and I cannot explain it.

pillar_controls.name: Bias & Fairness Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Measure bias across demographics; track fairness metrics; detect discrimination.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent may be giving systematically different answers to different user groups.

pillar_controls.name: Compliance Validation
pillar_controls.description: Map controls to SOC2/ISO/NIST; track implementation; conduct periodic audits.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I do not know which of our controls actually satisfy which compliance requirements.

pillar_controls.name: Decision Traceability
pillar_controls.description: Track provenance from input to output; document data sources; maintain chain of custody.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I cannot explain the data path that led to a specific agent decision.

pillar_controls.name: User Interaction Logging
pillar_controls.description: Log prompts and feedback; track satisfaction metrics; analyze behavior.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I have no record of what users actually asked my agent.

pillar_controls.name: Change Tracking
pillar_controls.description: Log configuration changes; track model updates and retraining; version control artifacts.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Something changed and I do not know what — the agent stopped working correctly.

pillar_controls.name: Vulnerability Scanning
pillar_controls.description: Conduct regular scans; assess threats (MITRE ATLAS); correlate with CVEs.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I do not know if my AI infrastructure has known vulnerabilities.

pillar_controls.name: AI System Registry
pillar_controls.description: Catalog all LLMs, RAGs, and Agents; document owners and criticality.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Someone asks how many AI agents we are running and we do not have a definitive answer.

pillar_controls.name: Model Catalog
pillar_controls.description: Inventory base and fine-tuned models; track versions and training sets.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I cannot tell which model version produced a specific output last month.

pillar_controls.name: Agent Capability Documentation
pillar_controls.description: Document agent autonomy levels, tools, and decision authority.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A new team member does not know what the agent is allowed to do.

pillar_controls.name: Data Source Mapping
pillar_controls.description: Map data lineage; track refresh rates; identify RAG dependencies.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I cannot tell which knowledge base is affecting a specific agent response.

pillar_controls.name: API Inventory
pillar_controls.description: Catalog all APIs and MCP endpoints; document auth methods and rate limits.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I do not know all the external services my agents are calling.

pillar_controls.name: Tool Registry
pillar_controls.description: Maintain registry of tools and plugins; track permissions and usage.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: An agent called a tool it should not have access to.

pillar_controls.name: Dependency Tracking
pillar_controls.description: Track libraries and frameworks; identify transitive dependencies; cross-reference CVEs.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A transitive dependency in my AI stack has a known CVE.

pillar_controls.name: Architecture Documentation
pillar_controls.description: Document RAG and MCP architectures and data flows.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: New engineers cannot understand how the AI system is structured.

pillar_controls.name: Risk Registers
pillar_controls.description: Centralized threat register; link risks to components; track mitigation.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have no formal record of the risks we have accepted in our AI stack.

pillar_controls.name: Configuration Baselines
pillar_controls.description: Establish baselines; track deviations; drift detection.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Someone changed an agent configuration and I do not know when or what changed.

pillar_controls.name: SBOM Generation
pillar_controls.description: Generate SBOMs for all models and applications; correlate with CVEs.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My auditor is asking for a software bill of materials for our AI systems.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Activity Logging
pillar_controls.description: Log all actions by service accounts and agents; track credential usage; SIEM integration.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A service account took an action I cannot trace back to a specific workflow.

pillar_controls.name: Agent Behavior Verification
pillar_controls.description: Verify multi-agent voting mechanisms; track memory state across interactions; SHA-256 state hashing.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My multi-agent system made a consensus decision I cannot reconstruct.

pillar_controls.name: Supply Chain Audit
pillar_controls.description: Verify OpenSSF OMS signatures; validate SBOM completeness against actual dependencies.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I signed off on a model but cannot prove it has not changed since.

pillar_controls.name: Memory Poisoning Detection
pillar_controls.description: Audit knowledge bases for poisoned content; detect adversarial trigger phrases.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Someone may have inserted malicious content into our RAG knowledge base.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Registry
pillar_controls.description: Automated discovery of NHI; lifecycle tracking from creation to decommissioning; fingerprinting.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I have service accounts that nobody owns and I cannot find who created them.

pillar_controls.name: Agent Architecture Inventory
pillar_controls.description: Document multi-agent communication patterns; track orchestration frameworks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My multi-agent system has grown and I can no longer describe its topology.

pillar_controls.name: Model Lineage Provenance Ledger
pillar_controls.description: Cryptographic chain of custody from base model through every fine-tuning stage to production; extends OpenSSF OMS.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I cannot prove which training data produced the model currently in production.

pillar_controls.name: Dynamic Agent State Inventory
pillar_controls.description: Real-time registry of every deployed agent with owner_of_record, ACT tier, tool authorizations, hear_agent_of_record, and control_plane_id.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Someone asks how many agents are running right now and the honest answer is we do not know.

pillar_controls.name: Semantic Execution Trace Logging
pillar_controls.description: Captures full agent execution trace: reasoning chain, every tool call with parameters, every memory operation — written to an append-only store the agent cannot modify.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Something broke in production and I have no idea what the agent was reasoning when it happened.

pillar_controls.name: RAG Corpus Diff Tracking
pillar_controls.description: Hash-verified change log for the retrieval layer; correlates behavioral changes to corpus changes automatically.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent started giving different answers after someone updated the knowledge base and I cannot prove the connection.

pillar_controls.name: Circuit Breakers
pillar_controls.description: Implement circuit breakers to prevent cascading failures; design graceful degradation.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: One failing upstream dependency takes down my entire agent workflow.

pillar_controls.name: Emergency Shutdown
pillar_controls.description: Define protocols for runaway agents; implement operator kill switches.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent is misbehaving and I have no reliable way to stop it immediately.

pillar_controls.name: Fallback Mechanisms
pillar_controls.description: Design failover strategies; implement redundant systems.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: When the AI is unavailable, my workflow stops entirely with no fallback.

pillar_controls.name: Error Handling
pillar_controls.description: Robust error handling in pipelines; fail-closed vs fail-open policies.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent fails silently and downstream processes act on bad data.

pillar_controls.name: Rate Limiting
pillar_controls.description: Prevent resource exhaustion; throttle API calls and agent actions.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A runaway agent loop consumed my entire monthly API budget overnight.

pillar_controls.name: Rollback Procedures
pillar_controls.description: Version control for artifacts; define rollback criteria.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A bad model deployment broke production and I cannot roll back cleanly.

pillar_controls.name: Kill Switches for Runaway Agents
pillar_controls.description: Implement kill switches with tiered authorization to halt agent execution.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I cannot halt a misbehaving autonomous agent without taking down the whole system.

pillar_controls.name: Blast Radius Containment
pillar_controls.description: Compartmentalize systems; define containment zones.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: When one agent fails, I cannot predict how far the damage will spread.

pillar_controls.name: Safe Defaults
pillar_controls.description: Design safe behaviors; validate assumptions; fail securely.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Under unexpected conditions, my agent takes the most permissive action.

pillar_controls.name: Incident Response
pillar_controls.description: Develop AI-specific playbooks; define escalation paths.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have a cyber incident response plan but nothing specific for AI agent incidents.

pillar_controls.name: Model Backups
pillar_controls.description: Regular backups of models and states; geo-diverse storage.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A model was deleted accidentally and we cannot recover the fine-tuned version.

pillar_controls.name: Data Recovery
pillar_controls.description: Procedures for AI datasets; point-in-time recovery.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our vector database was corrupted and we have no recovery path.

pillar_controls.name: Backup Automation
pillar_controls.description: Automate backup processes; monitor success and failure.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our backups were failing silently for months.

pillar_controls.name: Disaster Recovery
pillar_controls.description: Develop plans; define priorities; conduct drills.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have never tested whether we can actually recover our AI systems after a failure.

pillar_controls.name: Business Continuity
pillar_controls.description: Integrate AI into BCP; define minimum viable services.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our BCP does not account for AI agent availability as a dependency.

pillar_controls.name: RTO/RPO Management
pillar_controls.description: Define objectives; monitor achievement.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have RTO/RPO targets for databases but not for AI systems.

pillar_controls.name: Testing & Validation
pillar_controls.description: Regular recovery tests; validate backup integrity.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have backups but we have never verified they actually work.

pillar_controls.name: Off-Site Storage
pillar_controls.description: Encrypted off-site backups.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: All our AI artifacts are in one region — a regional outage would be catastrophic.

pillar_controls.name: Configuration Restoration
pillar_controls.description: Backup IaC; version control configs.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: After a failure, we cannot restore the exact agent configuration that was running.

pillar_controls.name: Incident Forensics
pillar_controls.description: Forensic analysis; root cause; blameless post-mortems.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: After an agent incident we cannot reconstruct what happened.

pillar_controls.name: Distributed Agent Fail-Safe
pillar_controls.description: Centralized kill switch for multi-agent systems; auto-quarantine of malicious agents; cascading failure prevention.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My swarm has no coordinated shutdown mechanism.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Revocation
pillar_controls.description: Automated credential rotation; immediate emergency disabling of service accounts.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A compromised service account is still active while I try to revoke it.

pillar_controls.name: Memory Poisoning Response
pillar_controls.description: Real-time alerts on RAG contamination; agent quarantine; compromised source isolation.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My RAG corpus has been contaminated and I have no automated response.

pillar_controls.name: Agent State Recovery
pillar_controls.description: Point-in-time snapshots of agent memory; version control for vector DBs.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: An agent state was corrupted and I cannot restore a known-good state.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Credential Recovery
pillar_controls.description: Secure escrow mechanisms for credentials; HSM integration for key protection.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A critical service account credential was lost and there is no recovery path.

pillar_controls.name: Agent Recursion Limit Governor
pillar_controls.description: Hard cap on tool-calling depth enforced at the API gateway layer — not the system prompt. Default maximum 4. Fail-closed.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent looped and spent my entire monthly API budget overnight.

pillar_controls.name: Swarm Quorum Abort Mechanism
pillar_controls.description: Decentralized threshold-based abort: when a configurable quorum of swarm agents agree the task should stop, coordinated shutdown proceeds without a centralized kill signal.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Stopping my orchestrator does not stop the worker agents it spawned.

pillar_controls.name: Behavioral Drift Baseline & Rollback
pillar_controls.description: Establish measurable behavioral baselines with defined probe sets; automated rollback when drift exceeds configurable thresholds.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent's behavior changed gradually over weeks and I had no signal until users complained.

pillar_controls.name: Multi-Agent Cascade Containment
pillar_controls.description: Limits the blast radius of agent failures in pipeline and orchestrator architectures; failed agents isolated so downstream agents receive clean error signals.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: One agent failing took down my entire multi-agent workflow.

pillar_controls.name: Human Approval Workflows
pillar_controls.description: HITL for high-risk decisions; define approval authorities.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent makes irreversible decisions with no human checkpoint.

pillar_controls.name: Explainability
pillar_controls.description: Provide reasoning chains; implement interpretability techniques.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Users do not trust my agent because they cannot understand why it makes decisions.

pillar_controls.name: Interactive Feedback
pillar_controls.description: RLHF loops; track feedback metrics.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent is not improving because user feedback is not reaching the training loop.

pillar_controls.name: Escalation Procedures
pillar_controls.description: Route alerts based on severity; integrate with incident management.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A critical agent alert was generated but nobody noticed because escalation was undefined.

pillar_controls.name: Real-Time Intervention
pillar_controls.description: Enable override controls; provide visibility dashboards.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I can see that my agent is misbehaving but I cannot intervene without a full restart.

pillar_controls.name: User Interaction Oversight
pillar_controls.description: Monitor for abuse and malice; track trust metrics.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Users may be deliberately trying to manipulate my agent and I have no detection.

pillar_controls.name: Red Teaming
pillar_controls.description: Regular adversarial testing; validate security controls.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent has never been adversarially tested.

pillar_controls.name: Risk Acceptance
pillar_controls.description: Establish acceptance procedures; document compensating controls.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We know about risks but have no formal process to accept or mitigate them.

pillar_controls.name: Collaboration Tools
pillar_controls.description: Shared dashboards for Governance, Security, and Engineering.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our governance, security, and engineering teams have no shared AI visibility.

pillar_controls.name: Transparency Reporting
pillar_controls.description: Report incidents and risks to stakeholders.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our board has no visibility into the AI risks we are carrying.

pillar_controls.name: Performance Dashboards
pillar_controls.description: Real-time monitoring; visualize KPIs and trends.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I have no single view of how all my AI agents are performing.

pillar_controls.name: Anomaly Detection
pillar_controls.description: ML-based detection of unusual patterns; tune thresholds.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent went off-rails for hours before anyone noticed.

pillar_controls.name: Security Logging
pillar_controls.description: Log security events; forward to SIEM; correlate events.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A security event in my AI system did not appear in my SIEM.

pillar_controls.name: Accuracy & Drift Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Monitor model performance; detect concept drift.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My model accuracy degraded without triggering any alert.

pillar_controls.name: Cost Tracking
pillar_controls.description: Monitor token consumption and budget quotas.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My AI costs doubled in a week and I did not find out until the invoice.

pillar_controls.name: Latency Metrics
pillar_controls.description: Track response times and throughput.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agents are getting slower and I have no baseline to compare against.

pillar_controls.name: Error Tracking
pillar_controls.description: Categorize failure modes; automate remediation.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Errors from my AI pipeline are mixed with application errors and hard to find.

pillar_controls.name: API Quota Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Monitor call volumes; alert on exhaustion and abuse.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A rogue agent exhausted our API quota and took down other services.

pillar_controls.name: Data Quality Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Monitor input quality; detect degradation.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent's retrieval quality dropped when upstream data changed.

pillar_controls.name: Compliance Logs
pillar_controls.description: Maintain specific logs for regulatory audits.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My auditor asked for specific evidence logs and I could not produce them.

pillar_controls.name: Multi-Agent Approval
pillar_controls.description: Human review when agents disagree; human gate for financial and system-level actions.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My multi-agent system makes high-stakes decisions without any human checkpoint.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Privilege Review
pillar_controls.description: JIT access for temporary privilege elevation; baseline validation for requests.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A service account has permanent elevated privileges it only needs occasionally.

pillar_controls.name: Distributed Agent Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Monitor availability and responsiveness of agents; track swarm topology and agreement rates.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: I have no visibility into the health of individual agents in my swarm.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Real-time NHI activity dashboard; detect unusual API call patterns and geo-location anomalies.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A service account is making API calls at unusual times and from unusual locations.

pillar_controls.name: Memory Poisoning Monitor
pillar_controls.description: Monitor RAG source integrity; detect embedding drift toward adversarial regions.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My vector store embeddings are shifting in ways that indicate possible poisoning.

pillar_controls.name: Adversarial Behavior Detection Pipeline
pillar_controls.description: Continuously probes deployed agents with adversarial inputs and monitors behavioral responses; attack patterns from red-team repository feed detection rules automatically.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My monitoring detects anomalies in outputs but misses the attacks that cause them.

pillar_controls.name: Tool-Misuse Detection Controls
pillar_controls.description: Establishes tool invocation baselines; detects tool squatting, unexpected tool calls, anomalous parameters, and invocation frequency spikes.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent may be calling the wrong tool due to tool squatting and I would not know.

pillar_controls.name: Emergent Behavior Anomaly Detection
pillar_controls.description: Classifies behavioral novelty — new task types, unexpected tool sequences, systematic decision bias — as security-relevant signals requiring governance review.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent developed a new capability I did not program and I only found out from a user.

pillar_controls.name: Jailbreak & Injection Telemetry Layer
pillar_controls.description: Unified logging and classification for all jailbreak attempts by technique: direct injection, indirect injection, cognitive injection, encoding bypass. Feeds E5.4 artifact repository.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Attackers are probing my agent and I only see the ones that succeed.

pillar_controls.name: Cloud AI Platform-Specific Monitoring
pillar_controls.description: Monitors Bedrock UpdateGuardrail and UpdateDataSource APIs; Azure AI Foundry configuration changes; platform attack paths that standard CloudTrail monitoring misses.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: The Bedrock Guardrail poisoning path via UpdateGuardrail is confirmed active and my monitoring does not catch it.

pillar_controls.name: Threat Intel Integration
pillar_controls.description: Integrate CVE and MITRE feeds; update threat models.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our threat model was written at launch and never updated.

pillar_controls.name: Playbook Updates
pillar_controls.description: Update response plans based on lessons learned.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our incident playbooks have not been updated since a major new agent deployment.

pillar_controls.name: Model Retraining
pillar_controls.description: Retrain to mitigate drift; incorporate human feedback.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My model is degrading over time with no retraining cadence defined.

pillar_controls.name: Patch Management
pillar_controls.description: Monitor and apply patches for AI frameworks and libraries.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: A vulnerability was published for a library in my AI stack and we have no patch process.

pillar_controls.name: Dependency Remediation
pillar_controls.description: Update dependencies; prioritize based on risk.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have vulnerable transitive dependencies that nobody owns.

pillar_controls.name: Policy Evolution
pillar_controls.description: Annual review of governance policies; align with regulations.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our AI governance policies predate the EU AI Act and have not been updated.

pillar_controls.name: Emerging Threats
pillar_controls.description: Procedures for zero-day threats; threat hunting.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have no process to respond to a novel attack on our AI systems.

pillar_controls.name: Capability Enhancements
pillar_controls.description: Invest in new tools; pilot security technology.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our AI security tooling has not evolved with the threat landscape.

pillar_controls.name: Performance Optimization
pillar_controls.description: Optimize latency and cost; implement caching.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our AI system costs are unsustainable at scale.

pillar_controls.name: Lessons Learned
pillar_controls.description: Conduct post-incident reviews; share findings.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We had an AI incident and did not capture any lessons from it.

pillar_controls.name: Operator Training
pillar_controls.description: Technical skills; security practices; incident response.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Operators do not understand the security implications of how they configure agents.

pillar_controls.name: Security Awareness
pillar_controls.description: Training for all users on prompt injection and privacy risks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our users do not know that their prompts can be used to attack the system.

pillar_controls.name: Safe Prompting
pillar_controls.description: Educate on safe engineering practices and jailbreak prevention.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our developers are writing prompts that inadvertently create security vulnerabilities.

pillar_controls.name: Incident Drills
pillar_controls.description: Conduct regular simulations and tabletops.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have never actually practiced responding to an AI security incident.

pillar_controls.name: Policy Communication
pillar_controls.description: Communicate policies effectively to stakeholders.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Governance policies exist but nobody reads them.

pillar_controls.name: Industry Sharing
pillar_controls.description: Participate in forums; share best practices.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We are solving problems the security community has already solved.

pillar_controls.name: Internal Documentation
pillar_controls.description: Maintain Wikis, FAQs, and Runbooks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Institutional AI security knowledge is in people's heads, not documented.

pillar_controls.name: Vendor Training
pillar_controls.description: Require security training for third-party vendors.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our AI vendors do not have the same security standards we require internally.

pillar_controls.name: Role-Based Training
pillar_controls.description: Tailored content for Developers, Operations, and Compliance.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Security training is generic and does not address the specific risks of each role.

pillar_controls.name: Culture
pillar_controls.description: Foster responsibility; reward secure practices.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Security is seen as a compliance burden, not an engineering value.

pillar_controls.name: Swarm Evolution
pillar_controls.description: Test new agent capabilities in isolation; improve consensus logic based on data.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My swarm algorithm is untested at the scale I am planning to deploy.

pillar_controls.name: Supply Chain Evolution
pillar_controls.description: Track OpenSSF Model Signing specification updates; align with ISO/NIST supply chain rules.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our model signing process is based on a spec that has since been updated.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Posture
pillar_controls.description: Optimize provisioning and rotation; integrate NHI-specific threat feeds.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our NHI lifecycle management has not kept pace with our agent deployment growth.

pillar_controls.name: Memory Defense Evolution
pillar_controls.description: Update poison detection algorithms based on new research; architecture reviews for memory attacks.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: AgentPoison and similar attacks have evolved since we implemented our defenses.

pillar_controls.name: Swarm Training
pillar_controls.description: Swarm Manager certifications; red team simulations targeting multi-agent systems.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Nobody on our team is trained to operate or secure a multi-agent swarm.

pillar_controls.name: NHI Awareness
pillar_controls.description: Credential theft and privilege escalation training; secret hygiene education.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Developers do not understand the risk profile of service account credentials.

pillar_controls.name: Supply Chain Culture
pillar_controls.description: Model signing training; SBOM generation and validation education.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our team does not know how to generate or validate an AI SBOM.

pillar_controls.name: Memory Awareness
pillar_controls.description: RAG security best practices; poisoning awareness and detection training.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our RAG engineers do not know that vector stores can be poisoned.

pillar_controls.name: Continuous Adversarial Evaluation Cadence
pillar_controls.description: Mandatory evaluation gates triggered by model updates, prompt changes, tool additions, and quarterly cadence — tests the current system, not the system as deployed months ago.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: We red-teamed at launch but the system has changed significantly since then.

pillar_controls.name: Capability Emergence Review Process
pillar_controls.description: Structured governance for emergent agent capabilities: Tier 1 document, Tier 2 security review, Tier 3 board approval, Tier 4 suspend pending investigation.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: My agent started doing something I did not design and I have no process to evaluate whether it is safe.

pillar_controls.name: Evaluation-Safe Pattern Library
pillar_controls.description: Validated reference implementations for all AI SAFE2 controls with platform-specific variants for Bedrock, Azure AI, LangGraph, AutoGen, n8n, and CrewAI.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Every team at our company is solving the same AI security engineering problems from scratch.

pillar_controls.name: Red-Team Artifact Repository
pillar_controls.description: Structured schema for all red-team findings; required deliverable from every exercise; integrated into E5.1 evaluation cadence for continuous reuse.
pillar_controls.builder_problem: Our red-team findings are in a report nobody reads and we rebuild every exercise from scratch.

cross_pillar_controls.name: Agent Failure Mode Taxonomy
cross_pillar_controls.description: Every agentic incident must be tagged with cognitive_surface=(model|memory|both) and memory_persistence=(session|cross_session) to separate ordinary prompt failures from belief and memory drift.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: My post-mortems categorize everything as 'agent error' with no structured way to distinguish what actually failed.

cross_pillar_controls.name: Adversarial ML Threat Model Integration
cross_pillar_controls.description: Mandatory governance artifact for ACT-2+ deployments mapping every threat with temporal_profile=(immediate|delayed_days|delayed_weeks|chronic) to capture time-shifted attacks.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: A poisoning campaign planted in our RAG weeks ago activated today and we cannot reconstruct the timeline.

cross_pillar_controls.name: ACT Capability Tiers 1-4
cross_pillar_controls.description: Four-tier agent classification system (Assisted/Supervised/Autonomous/Orchestrator) with mandatory control requirements that scale with autonomy level. ACT-3/ACT-4 require HEAR designation.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: My security review asks 'what level of autonomy does this agent have?' and I have no formal answer.

ACT-1.name: Assisted
ACT-1.definition: Human reviews all outputs before action

ACT-2.name: Supervised
ACT-2.definition: Agent acts with human checkpoints for critical actions

ACT-4.name: Orchestrator
ACT-4.definition: Agent controls other agents; enterprise-scale impact

cross_pillar_controls.name: Agentic Control Plane Governance
cross_pillar_controls.description: Defines the Agentic Control Plane — agent identity, dynamic permission enforcement, orchestration boundaries, runtime behavioral trust — as a board-visible governance concept. Protocol-layer meshes (A2A, MCP, ACP) must be evaluated against CP.3-CP.7.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: My board asks for evidence of AI governance and I have no structured artifact to show them.

cross_pillar_controls.name: Platform-Specific Agent Security Profiles
cross_pillar_controls.description: Per-platform companion documents (Bedrock, Azure AI Foundry, n8n, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) mapping SAFE2 controls to platform CVEs, implementation guidance, and monitoring telemetry. Protocol-layer meshes assessed as supply chain components.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: Generic governance guidance does not tell me what to monitor specifically on AWS Bedrock or Azure AI Foundry.

cross_pillar_controls.name: AI Incident Feedback Loop Integration
cross_pillar_controls.description: Quarterly AIID agentic incident reviews plus 30-day Incident-Informed Control Review (IICR) triggered by new relevant AIID incidents. Internal Agentic Incident Registry required.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: A major AI incident was published in AIID last month. I do not know if it affects us.

cross_pillar_controls.name: Deception & Active Defense Layer
cross_pillar_controls.description: AI-specific active defense: canary documents in RAG corpora, honeypot tool endpoints, fake credential traps in agent memory. First deception-class control in any AI governance framework.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: Every security tool I have is defensive. I detect attacks after they succeed.
cross_pillar_controls.first_in_field_note: First deception-class control in any AI governance framework

cross_pillar_controls.name: Catastrophic Risk Threshold Controls
cross_pillar_controls.description: Behavioral indicators triggering emergency suspension regardless of business continuity impact: unauthorized compute acquisition, communication outside approved list, weaponizable capability. CRT documentation required for ACT-3/ACT-4 deployment approval.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: We have no defined threshold at which we would halt an agent regardless of business impact.

cross_pillar_controls.name: Agent Replication Governance (ARG)
cross_pillar_controls.description: First governance standard for agent replication. Requires: replication authority in deployment manifests enforced at gateway; ephemeral credentials with scope narrowing per delegation hop; cryptographic lineage tokens; ACT-3 max 2 hops, ACT-4 max 3 hops; 500ms full delegation-tree severance on kill signal.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: My orchestrator spawns sub-agents and I have no way to track, limit, or stop the entire spawned tree.
cross_pillar_controls.first_in_field_note: First governance standard for agent replication anywhere. NIST, ISO, OWASP, and enterprise IAM have zero coverage.

cross_pillar_controls.name: HEAR Doctrine (Human Ethical Agent of Record)
cross_pillar_controls.description: Named individual with cryptographic signing key, real-time interrupt capability, and unilateral kill-switch authority for all ACT-3/ACT-4 deployments. Class-H actions require HEAR signature before execution. Fail-closed: no automatic approval path.
cross_pillar_controls.builder_problem: When something goes catastrophically wrong, nobody has clear authority and a fast enough mechanism to stop it.
cross_pillar_controls.first_in_field_note: First framework to define named individual accountability with cryptographic enforcement at the execution layer.

risk_formula.name: AI SAFE2 v3.0 Combined Risk Score

components.Pillar_Score: Organization pillar compliance score (0-100); higher is better
components.AAF: OWASP AIVSS v0.8 Agentic Amplification Factor (0-100); 10 factors x 0-10 each
